repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure: sharp ADE bounds from shared local-network completions under misspecified exposure maps is the closest catalogue item, but its kernel is graph-completion compatibility, not learned-representation distortion

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002 and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: interval/random-set partial identification already covers latent selections from feasible sets; the text/image representation-fiber ATE envelope is a domain-specific specialization

FongGrimmer2023 shows randomized texts alone do not identify latent-treatment effects; ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments and CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 impose stronger representation conditions rather than an epsilon-fiber sensitivity frontier
routine interval algebra for B_epsilon; no verified paper states epsilon-fiber invariance in this terminology, but the singleton criterion is not a standalone novel kernel

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The sign-reversal/impossibility framing names GenAI representation workflows but does not quote a published claim that a single endpoint is valid under only bounded representation distortion; ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments and CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 instead assume true internal representations or causal-latent separation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current kernel reads as a Manski-Tamer/Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari random-set specialization unless it adds a non-routine generic-class theorem or empirically checkable representation-calibration result.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The target m(r) is not fully specified once r is inverse-reachable through a positive-mass subset of its fiber: the proposal must say whether an intervention fixing R*=r also fixes a distribution over learned preimages Z, otherwise the envelope endpoints are not determined by the stated estimand.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The sketch says oracle sufficiency links m(r_j) to an average of observed regressions over a preimage set, but with the stated deterministic calibration c(z)=r_j, preservation of P_{Z,Y} instead appears to require compatibility pointwise on the selected preimage unless an additional stochastic selector/residual device is added.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a stochastic-calibration or intervention-kernel definition: for each target r, specify the induced law over Z inside F_epsilon(r) and rewrite Theta_I so the essential-envelope formula is mathematically forced rather than inferred from an ambiguous preimage average.

To lift beyond field/letter tier, replace the generic sign-reversal target with a named published estimator or workflow claim and quote the exact assumption being weakened; then prove an open-class obstruction for that workflow, not only a two-calibration example.

State a finite-support theorem first, with explicit selector variables and exact law-preservation equations, before the Borel essential-extrema conjecture; this would expose whether the interval is a true sharp identified set or only a closure/convexification.

recommended_next_step: Revise the estimand/calibration semantics and sharpen the published target; do not advance as flagship until the result is more than a random-set envelope specialization.
